page: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-mi355x-gpu is a bare metal server configured for high-density compute and acceleration workloads. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor built on the x86_64 architecture, providing 128 physical cores and 256 virtual CPUs with a dedicated allocation. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz and supports multi-threading with 2.0 threads per core.

The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For parallel processing and machine learning acceleration, the server integrates eight AMD MI355X GPUs from the CDNA4 family. These accelerators provide a combined total of 2304 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM, facilitating large-scale model training and complex mathematical computations.

Local storage consists of 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, offering high-speed data access for massive datasets. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This hardware profile is designed for resource-intensive workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, high-performance computing, and massive database deployments.

It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 128 physical cores and 256 dedicated virtual CPUs at a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Acceleration is driven by eight AMD MI355X GPUs from the CDNA4 family, which provide a combined total of 2304 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. For local storage, the server includes 28608 GB of high-speed NVMe SSD capacity. This hardware profile is optimized for large-scale machine learning model training, deep learning, high-performance computing, and massive database deployments.
